content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic Weekly Report (Apr 19 - 25, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The development focus for the week of April 19–25, 2026, centered on stabilizing the `plugin-anthropic` infrastructure to ensure reliable standalone publishing and accurate model integration. Key efforts included refining CI/CD workflows to support TypeScript package requirements and correcting model configuration errors to maintain compatibility with Anthropic’s API. These updates ensure that the plugin remains modular and easily consumable within the broader ElizaOS ecosystem.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### CI/CD Pipeline and Publishing Improvements
Work was completed to streamline the publishing process and improve environment compatibility ([#19](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/pull/19)):
*   **Workflow Alignment:** Updated CI/CD to gate releases on `typescript/package.json` and configured `bun publish` to execute directly from the `typescript/` directory.
*   **Dependency Management:** Replaced internal `@elizaos/core` workspace references with published versions to resolve `bun install` compatibility issues in CI environments.
*   **Compatibility:** Externalized `jsonrepair` to ensure better compatibility with browser-based Bun environments.

### Model Configuration Fixes
A critical configuration error was addressed to ensure proper model identification ([#20](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/pull/20)):
*   **Model ID Correction:** Fixed a malformed Haiku model ID in `DEFAULT_SMALL_MODEL` and `package.json`. The duplicated suffix "5-20251001" was removed, standardizing the string to "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001".

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** No issues were explicitly closed during this reporting period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there are currently no active issues tracked in the provided reports. The project focus remained entirely on PR-driven improvements and infrastructure stabilization.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The development activity this week was characterized by focused, high-impact technical contributions aimed at hardening the plugin's release pipeline. The work in PR [#19](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/pull/19) and [#20](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-anthropic/pull/20) demonstrates a commitment to maintaining a robust, production-ready codebase that adheres to the modular standards of the ElizaOS framework.
